message:
refactor(recorder_migration): deep cx threading — health_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Seventh caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase. Closes
the last two legacy storage call-sites inside recorder_migration.rs
`_with_cx` methods.

One prerequisite trait sibling added first:
- `RecorderStorage::health_with_cx(cx)` — default implementation
  checks caller cancellation, then delegates to the non-cx
  `health()`. On cancellation returns a degraded snapshot with
  `last_error: "health cancelled pre-start via Cx"` so callers
  distinguish cx-cancellation from actual storage degradation.

This brings RecorderStorage trait cx-first coverage to 100% (6/6
async methods: append_batch_with_cx, flush_with_cx,
read_checkpoint_with_cx, commit_checkpoint_with_cx,
health_with_cx, lag_metrics_with_cx). Tick 74 landed 5 of 6; this
tick completes the trait.

Two legacy `storage.health().await` call-sites in
recorder_migration.rs migrated to `health_with_cx(cx)`:
- `m0_preflight_with_cx` (line 363 — source storage probe)
- `m5_cutover_with_cx` (line 1094 — target storage probe after
  cutover marker append)

The other four migration methods (`m2_import_with_cx`,
`m3_checkpoint_sync_with_cx`) were already fully cx-threaded —
this tick completes the recorder_migration.rs caller migration.

Legacy `m0_preflight`, `m2_import`, `m3_checkpoint_sync`,
`m5_cutover`, `run_m0_m2`, and the underlying trait `health`
method all preserved unchanged.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 157/157
recorder_migration + recorder_storage tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
